name: fold command
guide_hints:
# Level 17: fold command

The `fold` command is an extremely powerful tool that allows us to expand or fold the definitions of logical operators. Most notably, the universal quantifier `‚àÄ` and existential quantifier `‚àÉ` can be transformed into each other!

- `‚àÄx P(x)` is logically equivalent to `¬¨‚àÉx ¬¨P(x)` (Everything is P means there does not exist anything that is not P).
- `‚àÉx P(x)` is logically equivalent to `¬¨‚àÄx ¬¨P(x)` (There exists something that is P means not everything is not P).

The `fold` command allows you to unfold a `‚àÄ` into a `¬¨‚àÉ¬¨` or vice-versa!

The syntax of the `fold` command is:
`fold <symbol> [<occurrences>] <target> [<out>] [<equiv>]`

- `<symbol>`: the symbol you want to unfold (e.g. `‚àÄ` or `‚àÉ`).
- `[<occurrences>]` (optional): the index of the occurrence you want to unfold. e.g. `1`, `2`, or `1,2`. If omitted, it folds all occurrences.
- `<target>`: the name of the formula you want to apply the fold to.
- `[<out>]` (optional): the name of the new formula to store the result in. If omitted, it modifies the `<target>` in place.
- `[<equiv>]` (optional): if provided, it registers a new logically proven theorem named `<equiv>` which states `<target> ‚áî <out>`!

Here are some examples to show how flexible it is:
- `fold ‚àÄ f1` : Modifies `f1` in place, replacing all `‚àÄ` with `¬¨‚àÉ¬¨`.
- `fold ‚àÉ 2 f1 f2` : Creates `f2` by replacing the 2nd occurrence of `‚àÉ` in `f1` with `¬¨‚àÄ¬¨`.
- `fold ‚àÄ 1 f1 f2 thm1` : Creates `f2` by replacing the 1st occurrence of `‚àÄ` in `f1`, AND creates a proven theorem named `thm1` which proves `f1 ‚áî f2`.

*Alternatively, you can just click on any `‚àÄ` or `‚àÉ` symbol in a formula on the screen, and click 'Fold' in the pop-up menu!*

Now let's prove our simple goal formula: `(‚àÄx x=x) ‚áî ¬¨‚àÉx ¬¨(x=x)`.

Step 1: Create the required variable `x` by typing `cv x`.
Step 2: Create the goal formula `cf goal (‚àÄx x=x) ‚áî ¬¨‚àÉx ¬¨(x=x)`.

Step 3: Create the left side of the equivalence as our starting point. 
Type `cf f1 ‚àÄx x=x`.

Step 4: Now use the `fold` command to unfold `‚àÄ` in `f1`, output it to `f2`, and save the equivalence theorem directly into `goal`!

Type: `fold ‚àÄ f1 f2 goal`
And you are done! It will generate the exact theorem needed to beat the level.

Note: The `‚áî` symbol is the logical connective for 'if and only if' (equivalence). You can type it by entering `21d4` and pressing Option+X (on Mac) or equivalent hex-code shortcuts on other systems, or simply copy-pasting it. The `<equiv>` parameter in the `fold` command automatically generates a theorem using this `‚áî` connective!
